findings.action: Replaced 'property rights' with 'economic stability' — avoids conflating ICESCR Article 11 positive entitlements with negative liberty framing. Also changed 'family stability' to 'family security' for register consistency.
findings.note: Valid observation. Article 11 protects adequate standard of living, not property ownership in the negative-liberty sense. 'Economic stability and family security' preserves the conservative-value bridge without overreaching.

findings.action: Added 'Research suggests' prefix to the voter lens claim. Now reads: 'Research suggests personal letters carry more weight than form letters.'
findings.note: The CMF citation already appeared on line 100 ('traditionally carries more weight'). Hedging the voter lens to match.

findings.action: Replaced 'mirrors' with 'echoes' on line 76. Conveys similarity without asserting structural equivalence.
findings.note: ADA (domestic, simple majority) and ICESCR (treaty, 2/3 Senate) share outcome-pattern similarities but differ in political mechanics. 'Echoes' captures that accurately.

summary.note: All 3 findings accepted and fixed. Scan-007 findings (4/4 addressed) + scan-008 findings (3/3 addressed) = 7 consecutive accepted findings. Healthy convergence. Build verified clean after edits.

claims.text: All 3 scan-008 findings accepted and implemented in src/pages/action/index.astro
claims.confidence_basis: Direct observation — edits made and build verified

action_gate.gate_note: No blockers. Next scan can proceed at convenience.
